methods.method_name: Measurement Equation Variance Differentiation by Study
methods.description: This method differentiates the measurement equation variances according to the study of origin for each data point. It is used to account for the dependency of measurement error variance on the data source, enhancing the model's accuracy by extending the parameter vector for maximum likelihood estimation.

steps.description: Modify the measurement equation to account for different studies as sources of data.
steps.searchable_summary: modify measurement equation to include study-specific variances

steps.description: Extend the parameter vector to be estimated by maximum likelihood to include study-specific variances.
steps.searchable_summary: extend parameter vector for maximum likelihood estimation

steps.description: Perform linear regression of maximum likelihood estimates of study variances on the year of publication.
steps.searchable_summary: perform linear regression on study variances and publication year

methods.method_name: Measurement Equation Variance Differentiation by Species
methods.description: This method differentiates the measurement equation variances according to benthic foraminifera species. It is an alternative specification to account for different origins of the data, although it is found to be less effective than differentiation by study.

steps.description: Modify the measurement equation to account for different species of benthic foraminifera.
steps.searchable_summary: modify measurement equation to include species-specific variances

steps.description: Extend the parameter vector to be estimated by maximum likelihood to include species-specific variances.
steps.searchable_summary: extend parameter vector for maximum likelihood estimation by species
